primary_accounting_issue: Déterminer le modèle IFRS 15 applicable à la reconnaissance du revenu d’une licence de propriété intellectuelle, selon que la promesse confère un droit d’accès ou un droit d’utilisation de la propriété intellectuelle.

authority_resolution.selection_reason: Applying the precedence rule strictly, IFRS 15 as the IFRS standard directly governs revenue recognition for licences of intellectual property, and there is no higher-precedence overlapping document in the provided context to displace it.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low — the question is directly addressed by the licence-specific guidance in IFRS 15, especially paragraphs B56–B61.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern whether a distinct licence is accounted for as a right to access intellectual property or a right to use intellectual property.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs define the licence context and clarify the threshold question of whether the licence is distinct or combined with other promised goods or services.
These general revenue recognition paragraphs provide the governing over-time versus point-in-time framework that the licence guidance maps into.
These paragraphs clarify the criteria and exclusions used to distinguish right to access from right to use.
These paragraphs support the measurement of progress when the selected licence model results in over-time recognition.
These paragraphs provide a royalty-specific overlay relevant to licence arrangements, but they modify timing within the licence model rather than creating a separate peer approach.

peripheral_authority.reason: These paragraphs are general control and over-time criteria not central to identifying the peer licence-specific models.
These transaction price and consideration rules are topically related to revenue contracts but do not identify the peer licence timing models.
Principal-versus-agent guidance is unrelated to the core issue of whether licence revenue is recognised over time or at a point in time.
These agenda decisions address other fact patterns and do not contribute to identifying the peer top-level accounting approaches for licences of intellectual property.

treatment_families.family: Distinct licence models based on the nature of the promise in granting intellectual property
Combined performance obligation when the licence is not distinct from other promised goods or services

approaches.label: Right to access intellectual property
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 15 identifies this as a distinct licence model when the customer receives access to intellectual property as it exists throughout the licence period.

approaches.label: Right to use intellectual property
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 15 identifies this as a distinct licence model when the customer receives the right to use intellectual property as it exists at the point the licence is granted.
